---
title: "ENVS 193DS Homework 03"
author: "Elena Donelson"
date: today
format: pdf
editor: source
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
---

# Setup

```{r}
# loading in packages
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(gt)
library(rstatix)

# reading in kelp data
kelp <- read_csv("temp-kelp.csv")

# reading in my coffee data
coffee_data <- read_csv("coffee_data 2.csv")
```

# Problem 1. Giant kelp fronds

## a.

You could use either a Pearson correlation test or a simple linear regression to determine the strength of the relationship between ocean temperature (°C) and giant kelp frond elongation rate (cm day⁻¹). Both tests analyze the relationship between two continuous quantitative variables, but a Pearson correlation measures the strength and direction of the relationship, while a linear regression tests how changes in ocean temperature predict changes in giant kelp frond elongation rate.
